entry.title: As a Manager or new hire, I should be able to take photos of I-9 documents directly SuccessFactors
entry.content:
As a  Manager or new hire, I should be able to take photos of I-9 documents directly through SuccessFactors so that photos dont have to be saved on work/personal devices or faxed to HR Support. Having I-9 documents scanned/faxed or saved on devices poses a security.

Acceptance Criteria: Enable the photo capability through SuccessFactors so pictures can be loaded for HR to review

entry.title: B1IF Scenario to create PDF file and use it in next step ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts ,

I have created a scenario to send PDF doc to API call .so first i create a pdf file using Crystal report atom then i convert the PDF to Base 64 using .net call .Here the problem what am facing is .net atom triggers before the file generated so the error is file not found .But i can see the file after the scenario end . also i tried delay login in .net but file saved only  after all process done .is thr any way to save the file in middle step .

Regards

jaba

entry.title: Re: Prevent reprinting the Payment Advice in F110 on S4HC?
entry.content:
Dear MED10,

Thank you for your question. Actually, the PDF icon in your screenshot is used for preview of the PDF file of payment advices, rather than for downloading. The output items section is mainly used for monitoring output request status and relevant parameters.

The printout of payment advices uses the backend printing function. In your screenshot, the status of the first entry for printout is "Completed", which indicates that the PDF is already in the print queue. What you need to do is connect the print queue to your local printer, and print the PDF using the printer. The PDFs in the print queue are only printed once. After the printing, they will be deleted automatically, and will not be printed repeatedly.

For more information, see Before Getting Started | SAP Help Portal.

Kind regards,

Sharon

entry.title: Re: Sap Customer Checkout Article sync error "Error while...
entry.content: Dear Gunther , Thanks for your suggested KBA 0003120095 and yes it has sorted my issues . Appreciated your help as always .Thanks and Best Regards , Tharindu .

entry.title: System health check
entry.content:
Hello all,

now iam looking to your help to found problems in my sap system at os level, like we use sm50,sm51,sm21,st22,st02,st03n t-codes for system health check at GUI level.

please give me some information how can i check my system at os level ....we use linux systems...

Regards

entry.title: Re: Manage Documents Feature
entry.content: Hi Parul, is it possible to store also Recruitment documents in the Document Manager tool of SAP SF itself? If yes, is it possible to have a direct storage interface without using onboarding module?

entry.title: Re: SMB Adapter for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
@Thimo 

Currently the SMB adapter supports Cloud runtime for publically available SMB servers and EIC runtime for on premise servers. Cloud Connector is not yet supported for the adapter.

entry.title: Getting 'ILLEGAL_PARAMETER_TYPE' error while trying to read an ADSO using RSDRI_INFOPROV_READ_RFC
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to read an ADSO using 'RSDRI_INFOPROV_READ_RFC' in an SAP BW/4HANA 2.0 system. 

We have observed that any ADSO containing a Characteristic field of type 'String' is failing with the error 'ILLEGAL_PARAMETER_TYPE'. The error is coming from 'fill_container_c  ' and the l_type_value  is 'v'. 

Does this mean RSDRI_INFOPROV_READ_RFC does not support to read ADSOs with complex data type? Is there any alternative to this?

entry.title: In which case we write groovy for removing root node&child nodes
Re: Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 (FPS02) - What's new?
entry.content:
I have a question on the load plan topic.

Its evident from the images that you are able to use load builder to plan loose product. I have only been able to get load builder to work with packages. For example, I can plan with any material defined with 
GenItemCatGroup: VERP, but when I try and plan with GenItemCatGroup: NORM it refuses to put it on the truck. 

We also found that cylinder packages on a pallet are displayed in the load plan prior to loading, but when loaded they are still displayed in the unloaded position, although the pallet and any box shaped packages in it are shown in the vehicle and it reads as if loaded in the hierarchy.

Thanks for any hints on this.

entry.title: Re: Stock Consumption of Packaging Material
entry.content: Check in OMS2 as to whether for the combination of plant and material type, quantity update box is ticked.  From your post, it seems, it is not ticked.

entry.title: Re: Standard BAPI BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE copy
entry.content:
The item document numbers are returned at the end of the BAPI (after the MB_POST_GOODS_MOVEMENT form has been run).
and so must have been calculated beforehand.

  • As I've always added my ZZ fields at the end of the standard tables, I've never had to solve this problem.
  • You could try to debug BAPI execution, defining a watchpoint on variable XMKPF-MBLNR or a break-point in  FM NUMBER_GET_NEXT ?
  • or you could use some PERFORM ON COMMIT and some assign to XMKPF record 1

entry.title: How to Enable Wildcard Filtering in Search Lists – Cloud Studio SDK
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on a development in the Cloud Studio SDK and have encountered a limitation in search lists. While I was able to add a new filter for search functionality, I noticed that it does not support wildcard searches using "*".

For example, instead of requiring users to enter the full value (e.g., "Material001"), we would like them to be able to search using a prefix or suffix with a wildcard (e.g., "MAT"). This would allow the system to return all selections containing the sequence "MAT" within them. However, in the current setup, users must input the exact ID to retrieve a result, making searches less flexible.

#cloudstudio #bydesign

entry.title: SF Compensation - Planner Audit for Salary Notes
entry.content:
We are using the Planner Audit functionallity and are facing an issue regarding Salary Notes.

We have activated the "Enable Planner Audit" under Settings and have selected 8 fields of our template to be auditable. For 7 out of this 8 fields, this is working well and we can see the changes when we open the Change History. However, for the field "Salary Notes", this is not working and changes are not shown. 

What could be the issue, what needs to be done to see the changes for Salary Notes in the Change History?

entry.title: Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
As SAP continues to refine its cloud offerings, the 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition brings a suite of enhancements specifically tailored for the Manufacturing line of business. This release focuses on enriching features across Production Engineering, Production Planning, Extended Production Planning, Production Operations, and Quality Management. Here's a look of the most important innovations of what's new:

 

Production Engineering 

Process Production Versions: Background Mass Edit

You can now perform background mass edit for the existing production versions. This type of mass edit runs in the background during which you can perform various other actions including mass edits for production versions.
You now have the option to select two types of mass edits:

Foreground

Foreground type does not allow you to perform any actions on the production versions until the mass edit is complete.

Background

Background type allows you to perform actions on the production versions during the mass edit. These actions include create, mass edit, lock, unlock, check, and delete for production versions.

This is the new button for mass edits: 

Process Production Versions - Mass Edit

Here you can see the new mass edit types: 

Process Production Versions - Mass Edit Types

 

Production Planning 

Manage Material Coverage - Planning Time Fence

The stock / requirements list of the Manage Material Coverage (F0251A) app now includes information about the planning time fence or manual firming date of a material.

Manage Material Coverage - Planning Time Fence

You can use a planning time fence to protect procurement proposals from automatic changes during the MRP planning run. If a planning time fence is defined in the material master data or for the MRP group a material is assigned to, the system can automatically firm the following types of receipt elements, depending on the firming type of the material:
• Purchase Requisitions
• Planned Orders
• Delivery Schedule Lines
The same applies to materials with a manual firming date. If the manual firming date is after the end of the planning time fence, the system uses this date to determine which elements are firmed. For materials with a planning time fence or manual firming date, the stock / requirements list in the Manage Material Coverage (F0251A) app now displays a separate row to indicate that a planning time fence or manual firming date is assigned, and to provide information about the corresponding dates. This way, you can easily check which elements have been firmed automatically, because their availability date is before the end of the planning time fence or manual firming date. If you want to adapt the manual firming date for a material, you can also edit it directly from the new row. The changes are immediately reflected in the stock / requirements list.

Capacity Scheduling Board

With this feature, you can now personalize and save a Gantt variant of your app. 

Capacity Scheduling Board - Personalized Variant Settings

This will enable you to save your specific settings for the app every time you want to access it. The settings that can be saved for a personalized variant are the Work Center drop down, Gantt settings, and Work Center settings. These settings are also carried over to the Order Details page and the Manage Operations page. 

Capacity Scheduling Table

With this feature, you can now retain the sequence of displayed orders after a realign operation. This can be done by selecting the Realign Sequence of Orders checkbox in the Realign Schedules window.

Realign Schedules - Retain Sequence of Orders

This functionality is only available when Insert Operation is selected as the Scheduling Control and Future is selected as the Offset. If you are realigning an order with this checkbox selected, to a date where there is already a dispatched order, then the Operation Start for the realigned order will be after the Operation End of the existing dispatched order.

Watch this short demo: 

Capacity Scheduling Table - Demo

 

Mass Maintenance of Products (DD)

With this feature, you can edit Product Specific Parameters on a product level by selecting the Edit button.

Mass Maintenance of Products (DD)

 

You can add these columns to the Product details page by clicking the Settings wheel and selecting the columns. The columns that are added are Product Spike Horizon Constant, Product Spike Horizon Multiplier, and Product Spike Threshold Factor.

 

Replenishment Planning Details

 

Schedule Buffer Proposal Calculation 

With this feature, you can override the default calculation for Buffer Level Zones using the Buffer Level Zone Update (DD_BUFFERLEVELZONE) Business Add-In. 

DD_BUFFERLEVELZONE Business Add-In

Once this Business Add-In (BAdI) is activated, you would be able to view the Custom buffer level zone calculation for products section in Step 3 while creating a proposal in the Schedule Buffer Proposal Calculation app. You can also view further details about the Business Add-In (BAdI) adopted proposals in the Manage Buffer Levels app. Check out this short demo video of how it's done: 

Schedule Buffer Proposal Calculation - Demo

 

Convert Actions in Planned Order (OData V4)

With this feature, two new convert actions, namely Convert to Process Order and Convert to Production Order has been provided with the Planned Order (OData V4) API. 

Planned Order - ODATAV4

 

You can use the above to convert a Planned Order to a Process Order or a Production Order. This feature will not automatically be available in your system but requires that you activate the feature Convert Actions in Planned Order (OData (V4) (S4HC_FTGL_PLND_ORD_RAP_BO) in the configuration activity Activate Features. 

 

Production Execution & Control 

Joule 

​Joule is now generally available and seamlessly integrated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition after being in early adoption. SAP’s AI copilot Joule leverages generative AI to empower users to execute their everyday tasks more efficiently and use natural language to express their business need.

The Business Objects in Manufacturing that have been enabled with 2502 are the following: 

  • Production Order Confirmation
  • Production Order
  • Production Routing

For further business objects that are on the way, please check this note: 3545050 - List of Business Objects Enabled for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

For those that want to watch two short teaser demos, here is the one on Production Order: 

Joule Production Order - Demo

 

And here the one for the Production Order Confirmations: 

Joule - Production Order Confirmations - Demo

 

 

Schedule ATP Run for Production/Planned Orders

The Schedule ATP Run for Production/Planned Orders app (F3456) has been enhanced and can now also be used for process orders. Therefore, it has been renamed to Schedule Collective Availability Check for Orders (F3456). 

Schedule Collective Availability Check for Orders

Items Based on ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Editable Co-Products and other Order Features

Enable modification of a production order or process order using a business object interface of the ABAP RESTful application programming model. You can now create, change, or delete co-products as order items. 

Enhanced Production Order Extensibility

Based on ABAP RESTful Programming Model, Post Production Order Confirmations with Goods Movements Using Proposal Data

Enable modification of a production order confirmation as a business object of ABAP RESTful application programming model.

Development Production Order Confirmation Extensibility

OData API Integration of Production Orders: Adding or Removing Serial Numbers

Enhancement of OData APIs for production orders so that you can enable changing production orders by adding or removing serial numbers.

 Enhancement of OData API for production orders for adding or removing serial numbers.

 

Quality Management 

Manage Certificate Receipts

This feature provides the flexibility to manually create certificate receipts within the Manage Certificate Receipts app, enhancing traceability and ensuring quality documentation is in place, even prior to physical goods receipt.

Check out this short demo for more information: 

 

Manage Certificate Receipts - Demo

My colleague Leonie has written a dedicated Quality Management release update blog post for all innovations: Quality Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edi... - SAP Community

If you want to have further details including watching our AI use case in action, please check out this Expert Talk: 


In conclusion, the 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition brings forth powerful enhancements that drive operational efficiencies and augment planning, scheduling, and quality management capabilities. This suite of new features within the Manufacturing line of business demonstrates SAP's commitment to evolving its cloud ERP platform in alignment with industry needs and customer feedback. These tailored tools will undoubtedly empower manufacturers to operate with greater agility and precision in today's dynamic industrial landscape.


📽 Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

  🔎 Find All of Our Assets   

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Planned Independent Requirement not consumed by withdrawal quantity in S/4
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

I had a PIR on February, being consumed by withdrawal quantity.

I deleted the PIR and created a new one (MD61).

Withdrawal quantity is still displayed in MD73 but doesn't consume anymore the PIR I've just created.

Any idea why?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Notification Cause and Damage Code Reporting
entry.content: Try IW69 with no filters but if you have it too wide open it may fail due to the data size

entry.title: Slow message processing sometimes - NAST
entry.content:
Hello guys,

our problem is that message processing is sometimes slow.

Most messages are fast proceeded like 0-1 second.
But then there are messages again and again, slow messages with 10 seconds, 1-2 minute, or more.
For example this is delaying printing for minutes.

Time span between ERUHR and UHRVR (table NAST).
Independent from media (print, mail, idoc), sendoption = 4 (direct sending).

In SQL cache the response time for queries to NAST are only milliseconds.
There is also no bottleneck for workprocesses.

do you have any recommendations where to analyze?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI Message Mapping Issue: Handling Empty & Null...
entry.content: Hi again, i created to mapping regarding your needs. You can take the part what do you need. If you provide me to sample XML and XSD's i can clarify more spesific. But If you create mapping same as mine it will be return what your expected behavior.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Environment in BTP: Handling API Timeout for Lon...
entry.content: As you are using a destination (cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( lo_destination )), you can also specify a timeout in the destination: go to transaction SM59, open the destination used here and then modify the timeout on the "Special Options" tab.

entry.title: How to resolve the error KI280 "Define a cost element category for account &1 (controlling area &2)"
entry.content:
When I do migration for G/L balance and open item I faced the error "Define a cost element category for account & (controlling area *) ".

Below G/L accounts are affected. They are created additionally by copying the exisiting accounts in YCOA. They all belong to G/L account Type "Non Operating Expense or Income "

Can you help me resolve this issue.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to resolve the error KI280 "Define a cost element...
entry.content:
The account being referred to is a profit and loss account, which means it is used to record revenues and expenses, ultimately determining the net profit or loss for a specific period. This account has been assigned to an object within the cost accounting module. These objects can include various entities such as a cost center, which is a department or function within an organization; an order, which could be a specific job or task; a project, which is a temporary endeavor with a defined beginning and end; or a profitability segment, which is a part of the organization for which profitability is measured.

When an account is assigned to any of these cost accounting objects, it is essential to ensure that a corresponding cost element category exists for the account within the designated controlling area. The controlling area is an organizational unit within the SAP system that represents a closed system used for cost accounting purposes.

To verify or create the necessary cost element category for the account, you should use transaction FS00 in the SAP system. Within this transaction, navigate to the 'Control Data' section. This area allows you to manage and configure the necessary settings to ensure that the account is properly categorized and can be used effectively within the cost accounting framework.

To resolve this problem, define a cost element category for account and post again or delete the auxiliary account assignment to objects in cost accounting.

With profitability segment assignment, the error message is only displayed when account-based profitability analysis is activated. Here it may be a good idea to suppress the error message and ignore the account assignment during the posting. This is particularly the case when the posting is not only relevant for the result from the usual business activity, for example for interest from cross-company sales in a billing document. Such postings should not be taken into consideration in the profitability analysis. In order to do this, you can define the account in transaction FCO_COPA_EX_N (IMG -> Controlling -> General Controlling -> Account Assignment Logic -> Exceptions for Postings to Profitability Segment).

Regards

entry.title: Re: DRC - no data selected by "Switzerland VAT return (CH...
entry.content: can you tell if you used the Report Definition: CH_VAT_DCL_2019_V0 standard or did you have to make an extension to use another field for the tax amount?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Environment in BTP: Handling API Timeout for Lon...
entry.content: No SM59 in BTP.

entry.title: SF Compensation - Executive Review: Filter Option Form View
entry.content:
For one of our Compensation Planning processes, we are using the Second Manager Hierarchy. In the Executive Review, with the Standard Filters, it is possible to filter by Team View (Second Manager). There could be cases where this is not reflecting the Form View (e.g. if a Second Manager has been updated, but the employee has not been moved from one form to the other). 

Therefore, we are thinking of activating the form view under Advanced Settings, which will disable the team view. In a KBA ("2084808 - Executive Review - Enable form view (This will disable team view) - Compensation"), it says that this is not a recommended option for clients. 

Why is this stated? What are the downsides of this option respectively what do we have to be aware of?

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with SAP SNC for RFC integrations
entry.content:
As things go mad with product naming, the CommonCryptoLib 8.x (CCL) is often still referred as SAPCRYPTOLIB and the archive shipping the CCL is still named SAPCRYPTOLIB_*.SAR.

If the archive name starts with SAPCRYPTOLIB_8 you are retrieving the CCL 8.x and are good to go. The deprecated SAPCRYPTOLIB 5.5 can only be downloaded from the archive section of SAP Software Center.

BR Joe

entry.title: Re: ABAP Environment in BTP: Handling API Timeout for Lon...
entry.content: I still have the issue even with `i_timeout = -1`. Are there any other parameters I need to adjust?

entry.title: Re: SAP on Windows server 2025
entry.content:
SAP has been testing its products internally on Windows Server 2025 for several months, but it needs to consider the recently released final version and perform final validation.

It is expected to be supported by SAP in Q2 2025.

SAP PAM is the official channel for verifying compatibility within SAP systems.  Once Windows Server 2025 is supported , the information will be updated in    PAM Tool.

entry.title: Alternate to Warehouse managed Physical inventory process
entry.content:
For WMS we use four apps to complete the physical inventory procedure.

Create - > Enter count -> Confirm count/Post -> Confirm differences

We have a requirement to reduce the user intervention and make a one step process for the same.

Is there any other way we can do the same process with lesser steps/is there any way we can have 1 single app to do all the processes?



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Alternate to Warehouse managed Physical inventory pro...
entry.content:
WM Physical inventory use movement type 711/712 to increase/decrease EWM stock.

It is possible to increase/decrease EWM stock with single step but the movement types can be different.

Option  1: APP Post Goods Issue - Unplanned (APP ID /SCWM/ADGI)

Option 2: APP Post Goods Movement (APP ID MIGO) but you need set customizing “Activate Synchronous Goods Movements in the Warehouse” to prevent delivery creation.

entry.title: Re: SAP Hana Express licence with more than 32GB
entry.content: @akullerThe upgrade options of SAP HANA, express edition, were discontinued a few years ago due to the very low interest in such upgrades in the ecosystem. Regards. --Witalij

entry.title: IBP OBP Custom attribute in /IBP/MARC_EXT
entry.content:
Hello,

In IBP R&S we are using SAP7 for planning areas based on external master data. In S/4 ECC the CUST_ATTR4 field of table /IBP/MARC_EXT is of type date, while in IBP we have an attribute of type CHAR(10).

Is possible to modify the data element of the CUST_ATTR4 field in table /IBP/MARC_EXT?

Since the other Custom attributes are already valued for other purposes and only this one remains on which we should be able to bring a char field (and not a date) into IBP. 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Integration to a BTP destination in SAP Build Apps is...
entry.content: Seems to be working fine to me. Getting 200 OK, screenshot attached.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Internal error: SQL View - Resource governor for 'cursors' exceeded
entry.content:
I am not familiar with this error, but from what I can read, it is using a connection to SAP HANA Cloud, DL RE (based on SAP IQ database technology) as a component of Datasphere. It should be related to that setting: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_DATA_LAKE/19b3964099384f178ad08f2d348232a9/a63e2b9784f21015983992c1d7a76694.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION

I do not know if you can manipulate a number of these temporary tables to reduce the number of cursors they open simultaneously. If not, then I would suggest opening a service case to SAP Support.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Thermal Printer set up in SAP Build Apps' apps
entry.content:
I am just using SAP Build Apps' Free edition, so did not explore any thing on BTP.

If you could help with any blog /  tutorial.

entry.title: MM EWM failed goods receipt
entry.content:
Hello,

 

There is an integration MM-EWM. We have the following situation: Purchase order was created. Goods receipt was done.

in EWM it was posted successfully (via inbound delivery)

in S4 there is no GR for the Purchase order. So, because of some reason, EWM failed to send GR to S4. 

Later stock was correct in S4 by movement type 712.

It was in the past and already does not meter the reasons of the issue.  So now we have Purchase order without any posted material documents.

The question: is it possible somehow to assign/update posted document to Purchase order? Because we need to create invoice and further things.

entry.title: Re: Dun & Bradstreet(DnB) integration With SAP MDG
entry.content:
Based on customer demand, Alluvion has built a SAP-certified SAP MDG Dun & Bradstreet connector.

SAP MDG D&B Connector 

You might want to look into this, instead of developing a connector yourself.

entry.title: Re: Thermal Printer set up in SAP Build Apps' apps
entry.content:
Hello Sankara

I just have data variables for which I need to get them printed, the data is being stored and retrieved from firestore.

entry.title: Re: CJ88/CJ8G-Controlling the amount of money to be settled
entry.content:
Hi SKnii

Honestly, I couldn't understand the scenario. It seems really strange to me.

In any case, there's no way to control the amount during the execution of CJ88/CJ8G, only through the settlement rule. It's possible to assign a settlement rule to a WBS element by amount. This way, for each settlement, you can create a new settlement rule with the desired amount, making the previous rule "inactive." After updating the settlement rule, you can proceed with the settlement execution.

Regards
Marcio Blos

Gurleen Kaur

entry.title: Re: SAP Hana Express licence with more than 32GB
entry.content: Hi @Vitaliy-R thanks for the info.

entry.title: Re: BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CREATE multiple account assignment
entry.content:
AFAIK you have to calculate it in your code (*), handle case of distribution (DISTRIB/VRTKZ) 'by amount', 'by percentage' or 'by quantity', just make sure the sum of the ACCOUNTDATA[] amounts and quantities is equal to the relevant values in the ITEMDATA[].

(*) But feel free to take a look at FMs such as MRM_AMOUNT_QUANTITY_PROPOSE*.

entry.title: Re: CDS Analytical Projection Views - the new Analytical Query Model
entry.content: @Kishore What exactly is the requirement? Which long text, where do you want to show it?

entry.title: Understanding Date Determination in Bank Statement Processing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Accurate bank statement processing is critical for financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. When an electronic bank statement is imported, the system captures multiple dates that influence cash flow, accounting entries, and reporting. The Value Date, Bank Statement Date, and Posting Date play significant roles in ensuring transactions are recorded correctly.

Understanding how these dates are determined from the bank statement file is essential for maintaining consistency in financial postings and avoiding reconciliation issues. Each date has a distinct purpose—while the Value Date reflects the actual cash movement, the Bank Statement Date represents when the statement was issued, and the Posting Date determines when the transaction is recorded in SAP.

This blog will explore how these dates are derived from bank statement formats MT940 and how SAP S/4HANA Cloud processes them during bank statement import. 

1. Key Dates in Bank Statement Processing

a) Value Date

  • Definition: The date when the cash inflow/outflow is effective.
  • Importance: Used for cash flow forecasting and interest calculation.
  • How SAP determines it: Extracted from the bank statement file, usually from a tag 61 in MT940/MT942 format

b) Bank Statement Date

  • Definition: The date when the bank issues the bank statement.
  • How SAP determines it:
    • In MT940, found in tag :20: (Transaction Reference) or :60F: (Opening Balance Date).
    • In camt.053, extracted from the BkToCstmrStmt node.

c) Posting Date

  • Definition: The date when the transaction is posted in SAP.

When an MT940 file is imported into SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the system extracts key dates to determine how transactions are recorded and posted. Below is a sample MT940 file, and how SAP interprets the relevant dates during bank statement processing.

{1:F01            0000000000}{2:O9401829041029LRLRXXXX4A0500004554351306031829N}{4:

:20:20230831125928

:25:011000390/33333

:28C:01295/1

:60F:C230831USD86324,20

:61:2308300829C100,00NMSC0815

:86:051?00TestSAP?204711?32X

:61:2308290828C200,00NMSC0815

:86:051?00TestSAP?204711?32X

:61:2308280827C300,00NMSC0815

:86:051?00TestSAP?204711?32X

:61:2308270826C400,00NMSC0815

:86:051?00TestSAP?204711?32X

:62F:C230827USD87324,20

-}

How SAP Interprets Dates from the MT940 File

  1. Bank Statement Date → Posting Date in SAP

    • MT940 Tag: :62F:C230827USD87324,20
    • Extracted Date: 230827 (27th August 2023)
    • Mapped SAP Fields:
      • FEBKO-AZDAT → Bank Statement Date
      • BKPF-BUDAT → Posting Date in the Document

  2. Value Date → Line Item Value Date in SAP

    • MT940 Tag: :61:2308300829C100,00NMSC0815
    • Extracted Date: 230830 (30th August 2023)
    • Mapped SAP Fields:
      • FEBEP-VALUT → Bank Statement Line Item Value Date
      • BSEG-VALUT → Line Item Value Date

  3. Posting Date at the Bank → Document Date in SAP

    • MT940 Tag: :61:2308300829C100,00NMSC0815
    • Extracted Date: 0829 (29th August 2023)
    • Mapped SAP Fields:
      • FEBEP-BVDAT → Posting Date at the Bank
      • BKPF-BLDAT → Document Date in Document

How the values are updated in the system:

Understanding these mappings is essential to ensure correct financial postings, avoid reconciliation discrepancies, and configure bank statement processing rules accurately.

entry.title: Re: comment tab in grc screen throwing error
entry.content: Did you already read this KBA 2948006 - WDA: Summary on how to check UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION dump raised in Application Component BC-WD-ABA* ?

entry.title: Incorrect "House Bank" and "House Bank Account" in Check Cash Flow Item app?
entry.content:
Dear SAP,


We have notived an issue that the journal entries displayed in “APP: Manage Journal Entries” are correct, but when I try to check them in “APP: Check Cash Flow Item,” they do not show “House Bank： SKSFK” and “House Bank Account： TTUMI, but are linked to “House Bank： GNMHD” and “House Bank Account： FUTUU”



Could you please verify why this is happening?


The details are in the attached document.


Best PERSON_NAME,


Fujita


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect "House Bank" and "House Bank Account" in Ch...
entry.content:
The house bank and house bank account in the cash management apps (One Exposure from Operations hub) are derived based on the House Bank Account Connectivity in app Manage Bank Accounts of the respective bank account of the transaction. House Bank GNMHD and House Bank Account FUTUU are assigned to bank account 0841228 in app Manage Bank Accounts, to which G/L account 11001000 is assigned. This is different from the house bank of the line items of the respective journal entry 100000975 since these house bank details were probably entered manually when posting the journal entry.

You can refer to the SAP Help Portal Defining House Bank Account Connectivity

entry.title: Re: How to deactivate cost center for cost element
entry.content:
Hello,

if a G/L account shall not be relevant for CO reporting/ CO account assignments (e.g. cost center or project), you need to use G/L account type "Nonoperating Expense or Income". This is used to create P&L accounts which are not relevant for controlling/ management accounting.

BR, Birgit

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud Integration with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@FernandoLopez 
In Case you are still looking for a solution:
While setting up the connection, I came across the same error as you did. 

I forgot the "-api" in the connection setup.

Have a good one!

entry.title: Re: Error in Data Action after activate Data Access Contr...
entry.content:
Thanks for the answers, we managed to solve it by applying a delay before executing the data action in the application, using the Timer function of the analytical application.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/142a436d0f2a4e97a9af15853ca9b739.html

entry.title: Re: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩
entry.content: Challenge Completed

entry.title: Re: Set up connection between SAP C4C (Sales Cloud) and SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @JoelleS,

What are the limitations of data flows compared to remote tables?

We always used data flows in the past and are not sure why to use remote tables for conneting SAP C4C and SAP Datasphere.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: ABAP Environment in BTP: Handling API Timeout for Long-Running Requests
entry.content: Try calling the Web service with Postman instead of BTP, to make sure the timeout is not coming from the called application.

entry.title: Re: SAP Translation Hub. ABAP Integration How-To
entry.content:
Dear @NicolasH and @VaraPrasadT, 
If you are having issues after following the recommended prerequisites for using the SAP Translation Hub application for ABAP translation as detailed in our SAP Translation Hub Neo or multicloud documentation as appropriate to the offering you are using, please create a support ticket under component LOD-TH detailing your issue and including your global and subaccount details and our team will be happy to help. Alternatively, you can email us at: translationhub@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Optimising your Probation Process using Performance Management
entry.content:
Good blog. 

I was just wondering, why would you send the form for Deletion in case of probation period failed?

Thanks.

entry.title: Which interpretation algorithm is used to extract the reference from FEBRE-VWEZW to FEBCL-SELVON?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I have a question regarding the Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) process. When the content from the <ustrd> tag in XML file ( format is CAMT.035 ) is populated into the VWEZW (Note to Payee) field in the FEBRE table, how is the reference then extracted into the SELVON field in the FEBCL table? I’ve seen on forums that interpretation algorithms are used for this. Is there a standard SAP interpretation algorithm for this, or how is the process typically handled?

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI Now Available
entry.content:
We are using Kerberos SSO at the moment > 20.000 Users. We want to move to this service smoothly. Right now we try that on our sandbox system. Unfortunately I have observed following difficulties, so far.

First Problem: On su01 only one SNC string can be maintained.

SLS SNC StringKerberos SNC String

Stuff like USRACLEXT are useless for dialog user SSO. This means the user could use either Kerberos or Secure Login Server and not both at the sametime.  I understand the non-repudiation and the cardinality issues, but anyway. still painful.

We need something like a grace period, where both could work in parallel. Not everyone has the latest secure login client and you can't roll out a SAP GUI within a day to everyone. Therefore you must manually track each user and change their SNC strings, which is basically impossible task for 20000 users. 

If both Kerberos and Secure Login Service Profiles are available on the secure login client, and the user did not authenticate to Secure Login Server yet. It will try to pick Kerberos and does not force the user to authenticate to the Secure Login Server, resulting in authentication error since the SNC string is maintained for the SLS and not Kerberos anymore.  (even if you maintain the GssTargetName on the registry) 

I am not authenticated to Secure Login Server and try to open the SAP System via the GUI 

It tries to use Kerberos instead, for which obviously i don't have a user anymore. At least the GssTargetName setting in the registry works and after manually launching the Secure Login Client in GUI mode and authenticating manually, which is a horrible User Experience, it works! 

- Removing Kerberos completely is not an option since there are other SAP Systems running on Kerberos SSO and they are not using the Secure Login Service. These are also obviously needed from our users, so Kerberos must stay there. 

Considering all this, This is no better than "username and password" login without SSO in terms of user experience.

entry.title: Re: scheduling unit tests
entry.content:
Although not a standard feature, there is this super nice project, which is just what we needed:

AUnit Runner for ABAP Environment

entry.title: Re: SSO Error in SAC
entry.content: It is posisble also to use IPS but using SAML mapping attributes for Teams and Roles is much simpler. The creation of users happen automatically upon login if you enable shadow user creation

entry.title: Re: ABAP Environment in BTP: Handling API Timeout for Lon...
entry.content: Is the method execute raising an exception? If not, you may be getting the response from an intermediary or the resource server itself.

entry.title: SSF Time Off - Holiday Assignment record update
entry.content:
Dear All,

we are creating a job in provisioning in order to update automatically the holiday assignments in Time Off.

We understood that there is only the parent entity "Holiday Calendar" in order to perform the import by template in "Import and Export Data".

The clients ask us the possibility to update an existing holiday assignment, changing the date field.

We have noticed that the holiday assignment date is the external code (key) of the entity, with no possibility to edit the record by template. If we try to change the date, a new record is created. Can you confirm?

Therefore the other possibility is to try to delete the holiday assignment record by template. If we try, we encounter the following error: "Delete operator is only supported for a base entity template." Is this mean that is possibile only to delete the "parent" entity (HolidayCalendar) and not the "child" entity (Holiday Assignment)?

Do you have any other suggestion to meet this requirement?

Thank you

Giulia

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am Carl, working as Process Excellence manager at allnex. We are slowly but surely starting a transformation journey with SAP Signavio, as part of a larger program covering the migration from SAP ECC to S/4 Hana. I had the opportunity recently to get my hands wet with the use of the different modules and functionalities offered in Signavio, and my first reaction is "how the hell am I going to find my way in all those functionalities and accelerators provided by SAP? how not getting distracted or sink! " 🙂

entry.title: Implico at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2025
entry.content:
As the energy sector navigates rapid digital transformation and sustainability challenges, Implico is at the forefront of delivering innovative solutions to optimize supply chains and enhance fuel retail operations. From April 8-10, 2025, in Rotterdam, we will join leading energy and technology experts at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference and showcase how cutting-edge automation and SAP solutions can empower companies to adapt and thrive in a changing market. 

We are proud to present two key sessions at this event: a workshop session on the Pre-Conference Day April 8 and a co-customer presentation at the main event on April 9, both featuring real-world applications of our solutions.

 

1. Optimizing Fuel Retail Operations with SAP Solutions 

Speakers: Jean-Marc Delbos (SAP) & Thomas Weggelaar (Implico) 

Efficient management of fuel retail networks is essential for profitability and customer satisfaction. This joint session with Jean-Marc Delbos from SAP and Thomas Weggelaar from Implico will focus on enhancing the end-to-end processes of fuel retail operations using SAP’s Retail Fuel Network Operations (SAP S/4HANA RFNO) solution and Implico’s automation technologies. 

 Key Takeaways: 

  • Unlocking operational efficiencies through SAP S/4HANA RFNO and automated data flows. 
  • Real-time fuel inventory monitoring and replenishment. 
  • Reducing operational overhead and carbon emissions through process automation. 

 

2. Automatic Trip Planning for Bulk Logistics 

Speaker: Adrian Mägerle (Migrol/Switzerland) & André Bente (Implico) 

(IDM) by Implico is a robust solution designed to enhance capabilities within SAP S4SCSD. Fuel distribution logistics is a complex puzzle that requires precision and efficiency. In this session, Adrian Mägerle from Migrol AG, one of Switzerland’s leading energy services providers, will explore together with André Bente from Implico how automatic trip planning optimizes the logistics in supply chain and secondary distribution by leveraging SAP-integrated tools and Implico’s Integrated Dispatch Management (IDM) capabilities. 

Key Takeaways: 

  • Reducing manual planning efforts  
  • Boosting efficiency and sustainability 
  • Insights from a prototype of the new Implico SAPlement AddTCO 

 

Enhancing Sustainability through Digitalization and the Clean Core Approach 

As an SAP SOLEX Partner, Implico’s approach to sustainability focuses on combining digitalization, automation, and optimized resource allocation with SAP’s Clean Core approach, which ensures that custom business processes are decoupled from the digital core of SAP systems. This strategy allows for easier upgrades, continuous innovation, and long-term system stability. 

By leveraging clean, structured data and the Clean Core methodology, Implico enables: 

  • Efficient transport routes that reduce fuel consumption and emissions. 
  • Real-time energy usage monitoring and sustainability tracking. 
  • Smarter asset utilization to minimize waste and maximize operational efficiency. 

These solutions are designed to meet both current needs and the challenges of a greener future, including the integration of LNG, hydrogen, and other renewable fuels into existing networks. 

 

Join Us in Rotterdam for Insightful Discussions and Live Demos 

We invite attendees to visit our booth for hands-on demos of our solutions and to attend our presentations to learn how we’re transforming supply chains in the energy sector. Whether you’re planning an S/4HANA migration or seeking ways to optimize fuel retail operations, Implico is here to help. 

 

Our Product Portfolio: Driving Efficiency Across the Supply Chain 

Implico’s comprehensive portfolio empowers businesses to streamline processes, reduce costs, and improve customer satisfaction.  

Learn more about our core offerings: 

  • SAP S/4HANA SDM (Secondary Distribution Management) - Optimizing last-mile fuel delivery through route planning, dispatching, and real-time monitoring. 
  • SAP S/4HANA RFNO (Retail Fuel Network Operations) - Enhancing visibility into fuel station inventory, pricing, and replenishment needs. 
  • IDM (Integrated Dispatch Management) - Automating route planning and dispatch to reduce downtime and maximize resource efficiency. 
  • PAT (Process Automation Toolset) - Simplifying and automating processes in bulk liquid handling, from loading to transport. 
  • Bottled Gas Management - Streamlining cylinder management, automating order processing, and optimizing distribution for maximum efficiency. 

 

Let’s shape the future of energy together! 

Schedule a meeting with our team:  

Visit implico.com to contact us or book a session during the event. 

 

We look forward to seeing you in Rotterdam from April 8-10, 2025!

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Date Range with timezone in UI5
entry.content:
Hi Vinoth,

The control itself can NOT be customised to work in specific timezone, but the framework can.  Here is an example of how the framework can be configured to UTC and the controls to work in the configured timezone:

data-sap-ui-timezone="UTC"

More information can also be found here:
https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/6c9e61dc157a40c19460660ece8368bc

entry.title: Re: Slow message processing sometimes - NAST
entry.content: You mentioned a difference between ERUHR and UHRVR, I assume the time difference of those fields does not match the delay in the output you experience? Or does it? It's a bit unclear from how you describe your issue.

entry.title: Re: Emergency contact mandatory rule
entry.content:
Hello @Khushboo ,

Kindly follow the KBA 3052127 - How to make National ID to be mandatorily entered by the new hire through a rule - SAP for Me.

I have made use of this KBA for National ID as well as Emergency Contact Info portlet successfully.

Regards,

Girish Choure

entry.title: Freight settlement in SAP TM for multileg
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using basic TM.

We have multilegs scenarios , covered in 1 freight order.

When it comes to freight settlement, is it possible to do settlement based on legs?

for example, leg1 is managed by a carrier A, we do the settlemet and once done, we do the settelment for the legs 2 which is another carrier. Is that possible or we need to have different freight orders?

entry.title: Re: Issue with Legal Entity Transfer for New Employment - from RCM to ONB
entry.content: Hi Łukasz - as we have the same issue now - were you able to check / clarify / solve the issue?

entry.title: Re: SAP Note 2001392 - new barcode technology for ZPL2 pr...
entry.content:
We installed the device type from zebra itsself (YZB200, ...).

see https://www.zebra.com/content/dam/external-dam/knowledge-base/What_SAPdevice_Type_to_Use_Rev5.pdf 

New Barcode-technology is supported.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA planning Cost Center and Allocations cycles
entry.content: Basically search for: transfer CO plan data ACDOCP

entry.title: Extend My Inbox Fiori Application for Purchase Order on SAP BTP BAS
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm extending my inbox PO approval app, the requirement is to add custom fields in master page in SAP BTP BAS.

 

Below are the fields

1.Supplier ID

2.Purchase Order

3.Landed cost

4.Vendor name

Can anyone please guide me on how this can be achieved, as this is new to me,

Thanks & Regards,

Atharva

entry.title: Connecting SAP Integration Suite with Microsoft Dynamics GP & SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently in the process of transitioning from the Smart Connector to SAP Integration Suite for integrating with Microsoft Dynamics GP. Previously, the Smart Connector facilitated seamless data exchange, but we are now looking for a more scalable and modern integration approach using SAP’s cloud capabilities.

Additionally, our invoices are managed in SAP Ariba, and we were using the Smart Connector to integrate SAP Ariba with Dynamics GP. As part of this transition, we also need to establish a reliable integration between SAP Ariba and Dynamics GP using SAP Integration Suite.

Key Considerations:
Efficiently replacing Smart Connector’s functionalities within Integration Suite.
Handling real-time or batch data synchronization between SAP, SAP Ariba, and Dynamics GP.
Best practices for authentication, data transformation, and error handling.
Leveraging APIs, event-driven architecture, or middleware for smooth communication.
Ensuring seamless invoice data transfer between SAP Ariba and Dynamics GP.
Questions for the Community:
Is SAP Integration Suite the best option for this migration, or are there alternative approaches we should consider?
Has anyone implemented a similar integration, and if so, what were the key challenges and solutions?
Are there any pre-built connectors, adapters, or accelerators available to simplify this transition?
Any step-by-step guidance or documentation that could help us successfully implement this integration?
Your insights and recommendations would be greatly appreciated! Please help us implement this successfully. Looking forward to your expertise on the best approach for this integration.

Thank you in advance for your guidance!

Best regards,
Changal Rayudu

entry.title: Re: LEVEREDGE 2024 Highlights: Discovering New Strategies for Digital Transformation
entry.content: Thanks for sharing your insights

entry.title: Re: Disable / Remove "Default" PODs in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content: Hello Istvan_B , were you able to find an answer to this as, I came across a similar issue and needed workaround for it .#Default POD @ManoelCosta @Kevin_Hunter

entry.title: Re: Report download to Excel and special characters
entry.content:
Have you created a real Excel file or a plain text file with an Excel extension (check with Notepad++)? In the latter case, I would guess that you and the other user don't share the same character set.

Please provide more information about the report type and the tool used to export/convert it.

entry.title: Re: SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI Now Available
entry.content:
It is possible to configure the ABAP system in such a way that SNC names coming from the Kerberos token and the X.509 certificate can be aligned. That way, they both fit the su01 definition. You will find details in the "Name Alias" section of this page: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP%20SECURE%20LOGIN%20SERVICE/c35917ca71e941c5a97a11d2c55dcacd/5888c2ee97f24e5c84f15e70487bcb04.html

entry.title: Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
With this blog, Ralf and I will walk you through below selected key highlights for Product Compliance in SAP S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition 2502:

  • Regulatory Content Updates for Product Marketability, Dangerous Goods Management, and Safety Data Sheets
  • Mixed Loading Check Rules for CFR 49
  • Mixed Loading Check Integration in Sales
  • Extension for Dangerous Goods Packing Instructions
  • Classify Partially Packaged Dangerous Goods

In case you do not need all the information below and briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video with the top three key innovations:

Video#1: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 for Product Compliance: Top 3 key innovations.

Regulatory Content Updates

Business Background

In the area of product compliance, the importance of content is crucial. Ensuring that products meet their target markets' strict legal and regulatory standards is essential, covering areas such as safety, environmental impact, and labeling guidelines. As businesses navigate the complex world of compliance, having access to comprehensive content becomes vital.

In this blog post, we will explore the significant role of content in product compliance processes, highlighting how it supports compliance with regulations and facilitates smooth operations. We will specifically focus on SAP's provision of essential content for various compliance elements, including compliance requirements, graphics, regulatory phrases with translations, listed substances, substance groups, and substance conditions. Join us as we uncover the significance of content in driving excellence in product compliance and aligning with regulations.

Key Feature

With this new 2502 release, SAP delivers new and updated compliance requirements for Product Marketability, Safety Data Sheet Management, and Dangerous Goods Management, which you can use in your product compliance processes.


Figure#1: Stay compliant with your product portfolio using the most recent regulatory updates

Regulatory Content for Product Marketability

In Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance, a compliance requirement contains data for a specific regulation, and this data is delivered as regulatory content for product marketability. In total, 274 compliance requirements are delivered for product marketability, with 150 including regulatory content.
With this feature, new compliance requirement versions have been released:

  • AU AIIC Status (Jul 2024)
  • CA Drug Precursors (Jun 2024)
  • CA DSL+NDSL Status (Jun 2024)
  • CH Chemical Risk Reduction Ordinance Status (Jun 2024)
  • CN IECSC Status (May 2024)
  • CN RoHS (Jun 2024)
  • EU Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • EU ODS Status (Feb 2024)
  • EU REACH Annex XVII Status (May 2024)
  • EU REACH SVHC Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP CSCL Class 1 and 2 Status (Jul 2024)
  • JP CSCL Exempt Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP CSCL General Chemicals (Jul 2024)
  • JP ENCS Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP ISHA Status (Mar 2024 Rev.1)
  • JP PDSCL Status (May 2024)
  • MY Poisons List (May 2024)
  • NL ZZS List Status (May 2024)
  • RS Banned + Restricted Substances Status (Apr 2024)
  • SG Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • US TSCA Export Notification (Jun 2024)
  • US TSCA Inventory Status (May 2024)
  • US TSCA SNUR Status (Jun 2024)
  • World IEC 62474 DSL (Jan 2024)
  • World PFAS Screening List Status (Apr 2024)

Regulatory Content Updates for Safety Data Sheets

In total 102 compliance requirements for safety data sheet management embedded.
With cloud edition 2502 delivered:

  •  Revised SDS document header/label phrases for DE Safety Data Sheet (Jun 2020)

In total 117 compliance requirements related to safety data sheet authoring:
With cloud edition 2502, 2 new compliance requirements delivered:

  • EU Specific Aerosol Labelling (Nov 2016)
  • EU Waste Code (Dec 2014)

In total 92 compliance requirements for occupational exposure limits.
With cloud edition 2502, 14 new compliance requirement versions delivered:

  • Bulgaria. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Canada. Occupational Exposure Limits British Columbia (Apr 2024)
  • China. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Estonia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Germany. TRGS Occupational Exposure limits (Jun 2024)
  • Korea. Permissible Exposure Levels (Jun 2024)
  • Latvia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Netherlands. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Poland. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Romania. Occupational Exposure Limits (Mar 2024)
  • Singapore. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Slovakia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jun 2024)
  • Slovenia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • USA. Workplace Environmental Exposure Levels (WEEL) (Jun 2024)

Regulatory Content Updates for Dangerous Goods Management

In total 15 compliance requirements for dangerous goods management embedded:

  • CFR 49
  • ADG
  • ADN
  • ADR
  • ANTT
  • IATA-DGR
  • IMDGCode
  • JT/T 617.3
  • NCh382
  • NOM
  • NZS 5433
  • RID
  • SANS 10228
  • TDG
  • UNRTDG

3 new compliance requirement versions delivered:

  • NOM-002-SCT/2023
  • CFR 49 as of May 13th 2024
  • ADG 2024

Packing Instructions for ADR 2023

  • P001, P002, P003, P004, P004, P005, P006, P099, P206, P207, P401, P402, P403, P404, P410, P502, P503, P907, P908, P909, P911
  • Intermediate bulk containers: IBC520, IBC620, IBC99
  • Large Packaging: LP02, LP03, LP101, LP102, LP200, LP 621, LP622, LP902, LP903, LP904, LP905, LP906, LP99

Mixed Loading Check Rules for CFR 49

Business Background

According to dangerous goods regulations, different products may not be transported together in the same transport unit. To ensure that only such products whose mixed loading is allowed are transported together, a mixed loading check is provided.

With this new release , a mixed loading check is provided for the 49 CFR regulation that determines whether compliance-relevant products can be transported together in the same transport unit or must be segregated. This feature can enable safe and harmonized transportation of dangerous goods and prevent environmental pollution.

Key Feature


Figure#2: Enable Mixed Loading Check for 49 CFR

The mixed loading check can be enabled using the Enable Mixed Loading Check configuration activity as shown in Figure#2 above. If enabled, the check determines the dangerous goods classification data for the products that are relevant for the check and compares the data with segregation rules that are specified in the Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations (BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD) Business Add-In (BAdI). This BAdI is released for developer extensibility. You can use it to influence the behaviour of the mixed loading check by specifying segregation rules.


Figure#3: Dangerous Goods Information – For the Value Chain app

You can view the results of the mixed loading check using the Dangerous Goods Information - For the Value Chain app as shown in above Figure#3.

Capabilities

Mixed loading check rules for CFR 49 supporting:

  • for Vessel: Segregation on Segregation Groups and Vessel Stowage other Provision of § 176.84, Column 10B of § 172.101
  • for Vessel: Exemption Tables of § 176.83(a)(4)(ii))
  • for Vessel: Class based Segregation of § 176.83(b), § 176.144(a)
  • for Road: Class based Segregation of § 177.848(d), § 177.848(f)
  • for Rail: Class based Segregation of § 174.81(d), § 174.81(f)

Extensibility

The new CL_BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD_49CFR example implementation is added to the BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD(Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations) Business Add-In (BAdI). This class implements the mixed loading check according to the 49 CFR regulation.

Related Information

For more information about the mixed loading check, see Mixed Loading Check in the product assistance for Product Compliance.

For general information about the Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations (BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD) BAdI, see BAdI: Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations in the product assistance for Product Compliance. For more technical information, see the ABAP Element Info view for the BAdI in the ABAP Development Tools. 

For more information about developer extensibility, see Developer Extensibility in the product assistance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#2: Mixed Loading Check Rules for CFR 49

Mixed Loading Check Integration in Sales

Business Background

The compliance checks in the sales document validate market assessments for compliance-relevant products and verify whether a safety data sheet is required for a product and whether the relevant safety data sheet is available in the specified languages.

They also determine whether the transport of a product is allowed or forbidden according to specific dangerous goods regulations, and whether products can be transported together in the same transport unit or must be segregated. Depending on the result of a check, the corresponding compliance status is set at both the item and header level.

Key Feature


Figure#4: Mixed Loading Check integration with Sales Order

With this new release, the timing of compliance checks that are carried out in sales documents, such as sales orders, has been changed. Previously, compliance checks were carried out when the Enter key was chosen in the process of editing a sales document. Now, these checks are carried out when the sales document is saved. Carrying out the compliance checks later in the process enables the mixed loading check to determine segregation requirements for all items in a sales order collectively.


Figure#5: New Blocked – Check Pending status

Since the checks are now carried out only after the sales document has been saved, the new Blocked - Check Pending status is introduced at both the item and header levels as shown in above Figure#5. This status is set when a new item is added to the sales document, or an existing item is changed, but the sales document has not yet been saved. When the changes are saved, the compliance checks are carried out, and the correct check status is determined and set.

For more information, please see Product Compliance Data in Sales Processes.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#3: Mixed Loading Check Integration in Sales

Extension for Dangerous Goods Packing Instructions

Business Background

In today's globalised economy, supply chains have become increasingly complex and interconnected. The transportation of goods, including hazardous materials, is an integral part of this ecosystem. Efficient and compliant packaging of dangerous goods is not just a regulatory requirement but a critical component of ensuring safety, efficiency, and reliability in supply chain operations.

Proper packing instructions are crucial for ensuring the safety of people, products, and the environment. Dangerous goods can include substances that are toxic, flammable, explosive, or corrosive. Improper packaging can lead to spills, leaks, contamination, and other hazards, posing significant risks to human health and the environment.

Key Feature


Figure#6: Extension for dangerous goods packing instruction

With this new release, you can view packing information according to dangerous goods regulations as a part of the embedded dangerous goods part III data in the View Regulatory Data - Dangerous Goods app.

Packing information includes header texts, references, and additional requirements, but most important is acceptable single and combination packagings. For combination packagings, the acceptable inner, outer, or immediate packagings are displayed, and when applicable, the maximum permitted quantity in each inner or outer packaging are shown per packing group. The page for packing information according to dangerous goods regulations is found via a link in the Enclosure Instruction Code column under Enclosure-Specific Data Sets.

Related Information

You can find more information in the product assistance for Product Compliance: 

  • For more information about the app that you can use to view dangerous goods regulatory data including packing instructions, see View Regulatory Data - Dangerous Goods.
  • For more information about the app that you can view packing instruction data, see Manage Compliance Requirements - Dangerous Goods.
  • For more information about dangerous goods regulatory content, see SAP Content for Dangerous Goods Management.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#4: Extension for Dangerous Goods Packing Instructions | Product Compliance

Classify Partially Packaged Dangerous Goods

Business Background

If a product has been packaged but its packaging does not comply with the applicable dangerous goods regulations, transport of this product is not allowed.

The new feature supports you, as a dangerous goods specialist, set products as partially packaged in the Edit Packaging Details dialog in the Classify Packaged Dangerous Good app.in preventing the transport of the partially packaged dangerous good.

Key Feature


Figure#7: Classify Packaged Dangerous Goods

When you edit packaging information in the Classify Packaged Dangerous Goods app in the Packaging Details section, you can select the new Partially Packaged checkbox for a dangerous goods that requires additional packaging before transport is allowed as shown in above Figure#7. Since partially packaged dangerous goods are enclosed only with an inner packaging, you only have to enter information for the applicable inner packaging. The dangerous goods packaging information is then no longer displayed when you select this checkbox.


Figure#8: Forbidden in transport permission for partially packaged dangerous goods

When you save your entries, the system automatically sets the transport permission for the partially packaged dangerous good to Forbidden as shown in above Figure#8. In addition, data that is not needed for partially packaged dangerous goods is no longer displayed. This includes further data and hazard notes in the Packaging Details section, and the enclosure variant and marking text in the Dangerous Goods Classification section.

After you have released the classification, it is used in follow-on processes, such as the transport permissibility check that is carried out in business documents within the value chain. This check blocks the business document from further processing and prevents the transport of the partially packaged dangerous good.

You can find more information about editing packaging details in the product assistance for Product Compliance. See Edit Product-Specific Details.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#5: Classify Partially Packaged Dangerous Goods

Quick Links

SAP Help Portal:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6540a57e42314b5a8c208bca283e29e6/679a1d3744d34b43832da6890b9095c0.html

What’s new: 

 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/fca11a9757664f39ba5fbca49bf5c5b9.html

Where to Find More Information

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

For more information about Product Compliance, please see the Link Collection Blog.

Resource Map

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Central Business Configuration
  • Extensibility
  • Two-Tier ERP
  • Automation Testing
  • Intelligent ERP
  • Master Data
  • Road to Success
  • Localization and Identity and Access Management

Recruit to Retire Human Resources

Idea to Market R&D and Engineering | Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

Acquire to Decommission Asset Management

Source to Pay Sourcing and Procurement

Lead to Cash Sales | Solution Order Management | Service | Professional Services

Plan to Fulfill Manufacturing | Supply Chain

Finance | Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC), Trade, and Tax

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!

From January 21 to January 24, 2025, we're hosting 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release including one session for Product Compliance. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. Don't miss this chance and watch the recordings on demand– anywhere, anytime.

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

🔎 Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Re: Analytic Model was created with older version of the editor
entry.content: Hi Mathieu, we face the same issue on some of our Analytical Models as well. We get the same error message, but I think the special characters (that we have too coming out of our source HCM System) are not the issue. We have these characters /MAN/ in almost every AM and most of them are still working. Our Analytical Models on PROD are also working (for now...). We face the error message only on DEV

entry.title: Re: Disable / Remove "Default" PODs in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content: Hi Anjana. Unfortunately did not find a solution yet. Still hoping for some solution from SAP

entry.title: Re: SMB Adapter for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content: @Swati_Singh Thank you! Meanwhile there is an SNote 3546814

entry.title: Re: FIORI Elements: Missing UI Message from Backend
entry.content: Hi @casualCoder, for this inquiry we recommend to open a ServiceNow ticket with the following component: BC-DWB-AIE-BOF. Our colleagues can then work on the problem more efficiently and try to find a solution for it. Best regards!

entry.title: Re: Which interpretation algorithm is used to extract the...
entry.content:
Read 3485355 - Customizing of Bank Statement in XML format. It explains how to check your current/SAP provided customizing and how to adapt it.

  • SM30 on VFIEB_MAPP_XCTRL - aka SPRO - Financial Accounting, Bank Accounting, Bank Accounting, Electronic Bank Statement, Configure Formats for Import (SAP Fiori), Define Mapping Procedure
  • STRANS - Start Transformation Tool

entry.title: Re: Plan Data Upload Starter - Directly upload plan data from a local Excel or CSV file via Story
entry.content: Thanks. This is very helpful.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting - New Balance Validation Process

Hi,

I am also facing same issue. Please let me know how this can be resolved.

Thanks,

Aarti

entry.title: Re: CRM Utilities - Authorization restriction in contract...
entry.content: Thank you very much Carsten.

entry.title: Re: Call web service error queryA ENOTFOUND
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am also getting the same issue while making an API call to the S4HANA on-premise system. Basically, my I the automation is trying the generate the CSRF token but it fails with the same issue - queryA ENOTFOUND.

API endpoint - https://sdcnl1dtm002.openstack.eu-nl-1.cloud.sap:44301/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PURCHASEREQ_PROCESS_SRV

In Postman, it works perfectly fine with the HEAD call and the corresponding csrf token is returned in the response headers. When I implement the same in SBPA with Ajax call (mentioned below), the issue appears:

Please find below the API call defination:

const options = {
    url: "https://sdcnl1dtm002.openstack.eu-nl-1.cloud.sap:44301/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PURCHASEREQ_PROCESS_SRV",
    method: 'HEAD',
    useWinCa: true,
    usePassport: true,
    responseType: 'json',
    headers: {
        Authorization: 'Basic ' + irpa_core.base64.encode('demouser' + ':' + 'xxxxxxx'),
        Accept: 'application/json',
        "x-csrf-token":"fetch"
    },
    async: false
};
 
return options;
 
I believe the issue is there with the Desktop Agent.

entry.title: Compass - Simulation - Currency conversion not detected as driver
entry.content:
Hi community,

I would like to simulate the effects of changes of the currency conversion. By that I mean a change of the conversion rate from EUR to CHF from 0,9 instead of 0,94 for example.

Description of my test Model:

  • I have created one measure (called "Measure_LC" for Local Currency) that is a currency type and the currency is coming from the Country Dimension (Organisation Dimension).
  • Based on that measure, I have a calculated Measure ("Measure_EUR") with a fixed target currency EUR.
  • In the Account Dimension I have "Revenue", which has no calculation and I dont have a hierarchy (Unit Type is set to currency)

Instead of having the possibility to change the conversion rate (eg EUR to CHF from 0,94 to 0,90) I can only enter a possible value in CHF and see the result in EUR (see screenshot). That is not what I am looking for (converting values is already possible in SAC without compass).

Is it possible to create the simulation that I have described? If yes, how?

Let me know if you need more information/explanation.

Kind regards,
Eric
